authoritative_sources.title: Elevating Salon Insights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PBA's KIM program is a recognized public beauty-industry benchmark reference describing the industry's first monthly salon metrics dashboard with transaction-backed KPI definitions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric definition, benchmarking, and salon-operator terminology intake
used_for: service ticket, product revenue, client, visit, service, and staffing metric terminology
beauty-specific benchmark selection and cohort caution
monthly trend framing for salon and spa operators

authoritative_sources.title: Powered by the Industry It Serves: ISPA's 2026 Big Five Statistics Show Steady Gains and Enduring Strength
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISPA's annual study with PwC is one of the only recurring public benchmark efforts focused solely on the U.S. spa industry and identifies the core benchmark indicators used in spa performance narratives.
used_for: public benchmark posture for revenue, visits, locations, revenue per visit, and employment
limits on cross-location and cross-format benchmark comparisons
trend communication discipline for spa narratives

authoritative_sources.title: Spa Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISPA's operations manual is a vendor-neutral workflow reference for booking, service-ticket, guest-history, retail inventory, returns, and provider-role terminology in spa and wellness operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Source-lineage intake and workflow artifact validation
used_for: beauty and wellness artifact lists such as booking records, guest history, retail inventory, and credit-card authorization
service-line terminology for spa, esthetics, massage, fitness, and retail workflows
reconciliation boundaries between booking, guest service, and retail artifacts

authoritative_sources.title: Barbering and Cosmetology Inspections Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR is an official state regulator for cosmetology establishments, and its inspections guide defines the beauty-industry evidence and control expectations around licenses, sanitation, foot-spa logs, and establishment records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence sufficiency, inspection context, and exception review
used_for: inspection and licensure artifacts that materially change location comparability
sanitation and establishment evidence expectations
refusal when beauty-specific records or inspection context are absent

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Spa Services Advisory
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Massachusetts DPH is an official public-health authority, and its advisory defines when medical-spa services require clinic and practitioner oversight instead of ordinary spa or beauty handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Adjacent-lane boundary and safety-sensitive comparability review
used_for: medical-spa authority boundaries
refusal when clinical or clinic-licensure facts are missing
routing to med-spa compliance or clinical ownership

authoritative_sources.title: Health Products Compliance Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is the public evidence standard for advertising and substantiation, which directly constrains beauty and wellness analysis that drifts into efficacy, appearance, or health-benefit claims.
used_for: claim substantiation boundaries
refusal for unsupported efficacy or health-benefit language
downgrading beauty analytics into internal-only language when evidence is insufficient

authoritative_sources.title: The Consumer Reviews and Testimonials Rule: Questions and Answers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Q&A is authoritative public business guidance for fake reviews, insider reviews, clear and conspicuous disclosures, and practices that can distort public reputation metrics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Review, testimonial, and reputation-metric control review
used_for: public review and testimonial handling
refusal when incentivized or insider reviews distort public metrics
disclosure expectations for review-derived outputs

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC MD&A is the authoritative public baseline for how KPIs, product lines, and material trends should be contextualized when analytics outputs feed executive, lender, or investor-style reporting.
used_for: KPI contextualization and trend explanation
segment and product-line disclosure discipline
refusal when beauty metrics are pushed into unsupported external narratives

workflow_stages.name: Service-line, jurisdiction, and source-hierarchy intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether the tenant operates salon, spa, wellness, membership, retail, or med-spa workflows; identify location scope, reporting window, jurisdiction, benchmark posture, and source precedence before analysis starts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Booking, POS, inventory, review, and licensure record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate beauty and wellness artifacts such as appointment books, service tickets, package ledgers, retail returns, commission records, reviews, complaints, inspections, and clinic or practitioner boundary indicators against named systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and denominator control
workflow_stages.description: Define visit, utilization, revenue, retention, retail attach, provider productivity, and reputation metrics while preserving service-line distinctions, package or membership logic, and whether med-spa or review controls materially narrow comparability.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system reconciliation and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile booking, POS, accounting, payroll, inventory, CRM, review-platform, inspection, and incident records without silently allowing a downstream dashboard or marketing narrative to override the operational source of truth.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis package assembly and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the report, metric dictionary, narrative summary, confidence posture, and action thresholds with explicit beauty-and-wellness evidence lineage, then stop when med-spa authority, testimonial controls, substantiation, or external disclosure approval would be required.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: appointment books, provider schedules, room or chair utilization logs, no-show and cancellation records
service tickets, POS receipts, refund records, package and membership ledgers, gift-card balances, and ecommerce sales reports
provider productivity, payroll, commission, gratuity, timekeeping, and service-mix records
retail SKU sales, inventory counts, purchase orders, vendor promotions, returns, and product complaint or reaction logs
CRM records, guest-history or intake records, reviews, testimonials, complaint logs, and reputation-management exports
inspection reports, establishment licenses, practitioner licensure rosters, clinic status records, and med-spa escalation artifacts
incident or adverse-event logs, claim review notes, board-deck drafts, campaign requests, and disclosure approval notes
public benchmark publications from recognized beauty and spa organizations
systems_of_record: document management or GRC evidence repository

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source hierarchy required for the analysis
prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytical briefs, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, and threshold summaries without overstating authority
escalate: required service-line, jurisdiction, licensure, med-spa, source records, or authoritative source context is missing and would change the outcome
public source guidance, tenant policy, and operating practice conflict materially about metric logic, review handling, or disclosure use
the request raises clinical efficacy, medical-spa, advertising-claims, finance-signoff, legal, or external disclosure questions outside the lane
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or metric definitions are unresolved
treat dashboard, review, or campaign outputs as sufficient when booking, POS, accounting, or licensure records materially disagree
approve public testimonial, efficacy, or benchmark claims because the internal trends appear favorable
state unsupported causal, forecast, benchmark, testimonial, or disclosure claims

domain_failure_modes: Treating beauty and wellness analytics as generic service BI work and failing to request service-line mix, provider-role, licensure, review-source, or med-spa context.
Collapsing salon, spa, membership, retail, and med-spa metrics into one denominator without recording the differences in visit grain, service duration, or package consumption.
Letting dashboards, screenshots, or social metrics override booking, POS, accounting, inventory, or inspection records without preserving the conflict.
Publishing reputation, efficacy, or customer-experience conclusions while review authenticity, complaint posture, refunds, incidents, or licensure context remain unresolved.
Using public beauty or spa benchmarks across incompatible business models, service lines, or locations without recording comparability limits.
Turning descriptive beauty and wellness trends into unsupported causal, testimonial, clinical, or investor claims.
Allowing the analytics lane to widen into medical-spa compliance, practitioner-scope decisions, pricing or refund approval, marketing-claims signoff, or legal interpretation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific scope, inspection, and med-spa rules vary across cosmetology, massage, wellness, and clinic models.
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The package uses a public baseline and requires tenant jurisdiction and service-line retrieval before practitioner-scope or clinic-boundary conclusions are allowed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenants may merge salon, spa, membership, class, and med-spa visits into one internal KPI.
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The lane requires the tenant metric dictionary, visit-grain logic, and override approvals to be retrieved and recorded explicitly rather than guessed.

notes: Authoritative beauty and wellness analytics records are the tenant-designated booking, POS, accounting, inventory, CRM or review, and licensure or clinic-boundary systems constrained by the cited public benchmark, workflow, and claim-control sources.
No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain for a validated spec pack because jurisdiction-specific and service-line-specific variation is handled as a required runtime input and escalation condition.
